title: tithymalus pumilus
intro: Tithymalus pumilus, also known as the dwarf spurge or European sandmat, is a succulent plant species that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its sap, which contains a toxic compound called latex that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, as well as exhibiting antiseptic and antifungal properties. Tithymalus pumilus is often prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or topical applications, and is sometimes used in traditional medicine for treating skin conditions and wounds.
